assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a webhook issue caused by API version changes or certificate issues after Kubernetes upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response provides 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks' commands to identify webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response offers two immediate fixes: deleting the webhook and patching it to be non-blocking

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and similar command for mutating webhooks

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response mentions these are temporary fixes, it doesn't explicitly warn about the security/validation implications of removing the webhook

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes checking current cert-manager version and upgrading to a version compatible with K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response recommends upgrading to cert-manager v1.16.1 which supports K8s 1.32 as the proper fix

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 2 showing how to patch the webhook with failurePolicy: Ignore

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response doesn't include specific commands to test pod creation after applying the fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention persisting changes in source control or deployment manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While response mentions it's temporary, it doesn't explicitly discuss risks of failurePolicy: Ignore or recommend reverting to Fail afterwards
